sections.heading: Welcome to NUA
sections.text: New Utopia Association Join the Association Learn More

sections.heading: About NUA
sections.text: Introducing NUA (New Utopia Association) Kicking off the 2026 new year with great news — a new association called NUA (New Utopia Association) has officially been registered and is now up and running! 🎉 Why NUA? To strengthen fairness, accountability, inclusiveness, and harmony among Utopia Owners, a like-minded group of owners has come together to create a new way of running our community — the New Utopia Association (NUA) . NUA was created by team of owners who share commitment to fairness, transparency, and harmony. What’s different? At its heart, NUA is a family of like-minded owners working together for a happier, fairer 🤝 community. Open forums 📢 and communication channels that welcome every member’s voice and encourage active participation. Well-defined byelaws with clear, enforceable rules for smooth community governance. Strong focus on legal and financial compliance ⚖️, with practices that reflect integrity and fairness. Fair sharing of welfare service charges between plot and house owners, based on transparent principles. Digital systems 💻 for communication, records, and voting to ensure ease and accountability. Quick Q&A What is NUA? A new, registered association for Utopia owners, built on fairness and transparency. How is it different from existing associations? No hidden charges, no irregularities, — Everyone contributes base charge and extra costs are shared fairly. NUA also welcomes owners from all phases of the layout, bringing everyone together under one association to strengthen unity and realize greater value for the whole community. Why join now? To be part of a modern, open, and fair community. Together with NUA, we’re building a transparent and welcoming association that increases value for members while cutting down on unnecessary expenses . What is Welfare Service Charges and how it is computed? NUA keeps charges simple: Every owner contributes a base fee (₹1250/month), and any extra costs (like staff salaries) are shared between plot 🟩 and house owners 🏠 in a fair ratio (1:3) as per best practices. As more houses come up, the per-member cost goes down. For example, if there are 100 units (80 plots and 20 houses), the total cost is split so that plot owners pay ₹1358/month and house owners pay ₹1572/month — ensuring fairness without overcharging anyone. A pre-calculated table is created and included in Bye-laws with staff requirements for easy understanding.

sections.heading: Guiding Principles of NUA
sections.text: Association Foundation NUA is formed by members with the shared commitment to foster a transparent, fair, and harmonious framework for community welfare and governance. Core Pillars Accountability in decision-making and financial management Inclusiveness in representation and participation Transparency in operations, communications, records and reports Harmony through proactive conflict prevention and members consensus Modernization Goals Clear, agreed and enforceable rules and regulations Digital systems for administration, payments, disclosures, and voting Participatory processes through open communication channels and discussion forums Our Vision A vision that prioritizes enforceable governance, member education, and dispute-free administration through transparent and well-defined processes and procedures.

sections.heading: Member Registration
sections.text: Steps to Join NUA Requirements Must be an owner in Utopia Layout Should value fairness, transparency, open communication, and cost optimization Should resign from the existing association by sending an email Membership Process Express Interest Email the NUA Managing Committee at nua-managing-committee@googlegroups.com stating your interest in becoming a member. Receive Membership Form The NUA Managing Committee will send you the membership form. Complete and Submit the Form and Attach Documents Fill out the membership form, attach the necessary supporting documents (like resignation mail to previous association, proof of identity and proof of ownership), and email them to nua-managing-committee@googlegroups.com .

sections.heading: Welfare/Maintenance Charges
sections.text: Calculator Scenario 2025-26 NUA Model Enter Details Association NUA Old Association Both (Compare) Plot Size 2500 Sq.ft. 3500 Sq.ft. 4000 Sq.ft. 5000 Sq.ft. Apply Annual Discount (Old Association System only) Additional Expenditure (₹ / Year) Includes STPs, Street lights, etc. 15,50,000 Residences Plots Calculate Charges Computation Results Graphical Comparison Scenario - 2025-26 This is a scenario for 2025-26 where the old association spent around ₹15,50,000 for additional expenses. In this scenario, the charges of both associations are compared assuming that all 213 units (Residences & Plots) are registered and contributing, with a 3500 Sq.ft plot size as the baseline example. Scenario Graphical Comparison 3. Transparent Service Charges NUA's welfare service charge model is transparent, scalable, and equitable , ensuring: Plot owners aren't overburdened House owners receive essential services Staffing is tied to actual revenue Charges grow logically, not exponentially Aspect 🌱 NUA Experience 🏛️ Past Experience Charge Philosophy Structured, Formula-based Arbitrary, year-on-year increase. Inflation is cited often Base Charge ₹1250 × (Plots + Houses) Not standardized Additional Charge Split 1:3 between Plots and Houses Shared equally, regardless of usage Employee Scalability Revenue-linked employee addition No linkage to revenue or unit count Burden Distribution Balanced — avoids exponential rise for plot owners Uniform burden — leads to conflicts Efficiency Measures Sub-committee study of previous expenses is used to arrive the structure. No expense analysis or consumption-based logic Outcome Fair, predictable, and modernized Inefficient, conflict-prone, and reactive The NUA Mathematical Model Unlike the old system which charged purely on Plot Size (Sq.ft. × 12), NUA enforces an equitable mathematical model prioritizing operational realistic costs and fairness between plot owners and residents. 📄 Welfare Service Charge Formula (NUA Association) Base Formula: Base Charge = 1250 × Number of Units (Plots + Houses) Additional Charge (if applicable): Additional Charge = Extra Expenses × Cost Split Ratio (Plots : Houses = 1 : 3) Employee Salary Rule: Up to 75 units → Max 5 employees, each at Rs 15,000/month Beyond 75 units → Add 1 employee for every Rs 25,000 increase in revenue (salary capped at Rs 15,000/employee) Why is the Residence Weight 3x? Residences utilize significantly more resources like STP, security coverage, regular waste disposal, and community water compared to empty vacant plots. The 1:3 ratio is considered an industry best practice among leading communities to ensure fairness. Key Benefits & Improvements Eliminates hidden costs and unexplained overheads. Provides a completely transparent breakdown of charges. Cost Optimization: The per-member cost structurally decreases as more members construct homes and move in!

pages.text: NEW UTOPIA ASSOCIATION BYE-LAWS (RULES AND REGULATIONS)

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 2 I. FOUNDATIONAL PROVISIONS 1. TITLE & APPLICATION: 1.1. NAME AND REGISTRATION: a) The AssociaƟon shall be known as the “New Utopia AssociaƟon” , hereinaŌer referred to as “NUA”, and is consƟtuted under the provisions of the Karnataka SocieƟes RegistraƟon Act, 1960 in Sep 2025. b) The registered oﬃce of the AssociaƟon shall be located at: Utopia, Tarabanahalli Village, next to Olde Bangalore Resort and Wellness Centre, Chikkajala Post, Bangalore -562157 1.2. PURPOSE, OBJECTIVES AND GUIDING PRINCIPLES: a) NUA is formed by members with the shared commitment to foster a transparent, fair, and harmonious framework for community welfare and governance. b) The AssociaƟon is founded on the principles of: (i) Accountability in decision-making and ﬁnancial management (ii) Inclusiveness in representaƟon and parƟcipaƟon (iii) Transparency in operaƟons, communicaƟons, records and reports (iv) Harmony through proacƟve conﬂict prevenƟon and members consensus c) NUA shall strive to modernize community welfare and governance, management through: (i) Clear, agreed and enforceable rules and regulaƟons (ii) Digital systems for administraƟon, payments, disclosures, and voƟng (iii) ParƟcipatory processes through open communicaƟon channels and discussion forums that empower all members d) NUA shall represent a vision — one that prioriƟzes enforceable governance, member educaƟon, and dispute-free administraƟon through transparent and well-deﬁned processes and procedures. 1.3. TERRITORIAL SCOPE: The operaƟonal acƟviƟes of the AssociaƟon shall extend throughout Bangalore Urban District. 1.4. APPLICABILITY OF BYE-LAWS: These Bye-Laws shall apply to all current and future members (residents, plot owners), tenants, lessees and occupants of the layout governed by NUA, including those who have entered into Developer Agreements with M/s. Ventures Build Tech, as referenced in Clause (d) of Para 6 and Para 10 of said agreement.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 3 1.5. BINDING NATURE OF RULES: These Bye-Laws shall apply to all current and future members (residents, plot owners), tenants, lessees, and occupants of any site, plot, or house within the layout who have formally enrolled as members of the New Utopia AssociaƟon (NUA). 1.6. INTER-ASSOCIATION RELATIONS: a) NUA shall endeavour to maintain cordial and cooperaƟve relaƟons with other associaƟons in the surrounding areas, including but not limited to Phase 1, Phase 2 and Phase 3 UTOPIA layouts based on Service Agreements if any. b) While fostering mutual respect and collaboraƟon on maƩers of shared interests — such as infrastructure, security, and community welfare — NUA shall remain autonomous in its decision-making and shall not compromise on its core principles of transparency, integrity, and members safety & interests. 2. DEFINITIONS Unless the context requires otherwise, the following terms shall have the meanings assigned to them in these Rules and RegulaƟons: 2.1. AssociaƟon / NUA “AssociaƟon” or “NUA” refers to the New Utopia AssociaƟon, consƟtuted by legally eligible site/plot/house owners of Utopia Layout(s) who have formally enrolled as members of NUA for the purpose of community welfare, governance, and upkeep, acƟng collecƟvely under these Bye-Laws. 2.2. Layout “Layout” means the Utopia Layout comprising residenƟally converted lands in Sy. Nos. 106, 107/1 & 2, 108/1, 3 & 5, 109/1, 2 & 3, 113/1–5, 114/1A, 1B & 2, 115/1 & 2, 124 (Tarabanahalli) and Sy. Nos. 38/1, 50/1, 51, 52/1 & 52/2 (Gadenahalli), measuring approximately 65 acres across Phases 1, 2, and 3, including all common ameniƟes and infrastructure. 2.3. Plot / Site “Plot” or “Site” means a demarcated parcel of land within the Layout, owned by one or more persons, intended solely for residenƟal use. 2.4. Commercial Plot “Plot” or “Site” also means a demarcated parcel of land within the Layout, owned by one or more persons, intended solely for commercial use. 2.5. Person “Person” includes an individual, ﬁrm, company, Hindu Undivided Family (HUF), or any legal enƟty capable of owning property with valid Ɵtle under applicable law. 2.6. Resident / Site Owner “Resident”/ “Site Owner” refers to a person who owns a house or site within the Layout, intended for residenƟal use, regardless of occupancy status.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 4 2.7. Member “Member” means a Resident/Site Owner who has been formally admiƩed into the AssociaƟon, whose name is entered in the Membership Register, and who agrees to abide by these Bye-Laws. 2.8. Proxy “Proxy” means a person authorized in wriƟng/email by a member to represent him/her in meeƟngs or voƟng, subject to condiƟons laid out in these Rules and RegulaƟons. 2.9. General Body “General Body” means the collecƟve body of all Members of the AssociaƟon, empowered to make decisions through duly convened (electronic and/or non-electronic) meeƟngs. 2.10. Managing CommiƩee “Managing CommiƩee” refers to the elected body entrusted with the day-to-day administraƟon and implementaƟon of the AssociaƟon’s objecƟves, rules and regulaƟons as deﬁned in these Bye-Laws. 2.11. Common Areas and FaciliƟes Includes but is not limited to: a) Roads, parks, gardens, open spaces, lawns, and parking areas b) Tanks, pumps, motors, generators, ducts, and shared installaƟons c) Central services (e.g., electricity, water, sewerage) d) Any other shared infrastructure necessary for safety, welfare, or convenience 2.12. Common Expenses Includes: a) Salaries, wages, and administraƟve costs b) Welfare of shared services (e.g., plumbing, electrical, sanitaƟon) c) Repairs and replacements of common infrastructure d) Any other expenses classiﬁed/approved by the General Body as common expenses 2.13. Common Proﬁts Refers to the surplus amount aŌer deducƟng common expenses. 2.14. Property “Property” means the enƟre Layout including individual sites with valid Ɵtles, common areas, improvements, structures, and all appurtenant rights and assets.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 5 2.15. Parking Span “Parking Span” refers to designated open areas within the Layout earmarked by the AssociaƟon for vehicles (2 & 4 Wheelers) parking, speciﬁcally excluding heavy duty vehicles such as trucks and buses etc. 2.16. Goods and Services As deﬁned under the Sale of Goods and Services Act and other applicable laws, includes all tangible and intangible items procured or provided by the AssociaƟon. 2.17. Majority of Owners Means members represenƟng at least 51% of the total plot/site/resident owners 2.18. VoƟng Window Refers to the deﬁned period (e.g., 7 Days) during which members may cast votes electronically or non-electronically. 2.19. Welfare Service Fund A fund consƟtuted from Member contribuƟons to cover rouƟne repairs and upkeep of common infrastructure and faciliƟes. 2.20. Corpus Fund A fund collected from members to ﬁnance major acƟviƟes such as repairs, renovaƟons, or replacements as and when required. 2.21. AGBM / EGBM “AGBM” refers to the Annual General Body MeeƟng; “EGBM” refers to an Extra Ordinary General Body MeeƟng convened for speciﬁc purposes. 2.22. Singular and Gender Usage Words in singular include plural and vice versa. Masculine gender includes feminine and neutral genders wherever applicable. II. MEMBERSHIP , VOTING AND MEETINGS 3. MEMBERS OF THE ASSOCIATION: 3.1. MEMBERSHIP: a) Any person/corporate enƟty who purchases or inherits a site/plot/house in the “Utopia Layout(s)” and becomes a member of the New Utopia AssociaƟon. b) Single AssociaƟon Rule – Membership is exclusive. A person shall not simultaneously be a member of any other associaƟon of a neighbouring/adjoining layout for the same site/plot/house. In the event that a member possesses more than one membership in the associaƟons for the same site/plot/house, all dues shall be recovered and the acƟons as deemed ﬁt shall be taken. c) If joining from another associaƟon, the applicant must submit:

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 6 (i) Either electronic/non-electronic acceptance of resigna Ɵon or ResignaƟon/DisassociaƟon mail sent to Managing CommiƩee of the other associaƟon, and (ii) Acceptance of such membership shall be at the discreƟon of the NUA Managing CommiƩee aŌer verifying the above and site/plot/ownership records. (iii) Transfer of Ownership – On sale, giŌ, or transfer, the new owner automaƟcally becomes a member, provided: a) The outgoing owner obtains a No Dues CerƟﬁcate (NOC) from the AssociaƟon within 7 days of request, if all dues are cleared. b) The seller and/or buyer pays all outstanding service charges, plus any transfer fee determined by the AssociaƟon (minimum ₹10,000). c) Membership is given only upon producƟon of the NOC and Formal agreement of memorandum of associaƟon and Bye-Laws of NUA. d) Inheritance – On death of a member, ownership and membership transfer to the legal heir(s) or nominee(s) upon producƟon of required legal documents. A guardian may be appointed for minors. Formal agreement of memorandum of associaƟon and Bye-Laws of NUA shall be required. 3.2. NOMINATION: a) Any individual member may nominate a person to inherit membership rights upon their death. b) NominaƟons may be altered or revoked by electronic/non-electronic request to the Secretary. c) All nominaƟons and changes shall be recorded in the Register of NominaƟon within 7 days of Managing CommiƩee approval. d) If the nominee is a minor, a guardian must be appointed. 3.3. JOINT OWNERS: a) Where a property has joint owners, only the ﬁrst-named owner in the AssociaƟon’s Membership Register has the right to vote and contest in elecƟons including oﬃce bearers. b) Joint owners shall be allowed to aƩend the Annual General Body MeeƟng & Extra Ordinary General Body MeeƟng without voƟng rights or taking part in any discussion. c) Primary owner can relinquish the rights of voƟng, parƟcipaƟng in discussions and also contesƟng in elecƟons excluding oﬃce bearers to any one of the joint owners sending an electronic or non-electronic noƟﬁcaƟon to the President/Secretary. d) On their death, the surviving joint owner(s) may request transfer of membership by submiƫng the death cerƟﬁcate and compleƟng formaliƟes.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 7 3.4. CORPORATE AND INSTITUTIONAL OWNERS: a) Firms, companies, or associaƟons owning property shall nominate one authorised representaƟve by board resoluƟon or legal declaraƟon. b) This representaƟve’s name shall be entered in the Membership Register and she/he shall exercise the voƟng rights on behalf of the enƟty. c) The owner enƟty may revoke or replace its nominee at any Ɵme by electronic/non- electronic request. 3.5. RESIGNATION OF MEMBERSHIP: a) A member may resign by submiƫng an electronic/non-electronic resignaƟon leƩer to the President (or Secretary if President is unavailable), staƟng the eﬀecƟve date. b) The Managing CommiƩee shall accept/respond to the resignaƟon within 15 days. c) Any outstanding dues must be seƩled by the member before the resignaƟon is accepted. d) Upon acceptance, the member’s name shall be removed from the Membership Register. 4. VOTING, PROXIES, QUORUM AND MEETINGS: 4.1. VOTING: Each site/plot/house, whether jointly owned or otherwise shall be enƟtled for one vote irrespecƟve of dimensions of the site. VoƟng rights are subject to compliance with ﬁnancial obligaƟons as deﬁned in Bye Laws. 4.2. PROXY: a) Member who is unable to aƩend himself / herself at the meeƟng shall be permiƩed to authorize another person (18+ Age) to aƩend the meeƟng. b) The electronic/non-electronic authorizaƟon, using the deﬁned AuthorizaƟon Form, shall be addressed to the President or Secretary of the AssociaƟon and shall remain in force for the Ɵme menƟoned in the AuthorizaƟon Form. The role of authorized Proxy shall be restricted to the extent of only for casƟng of vote on behalf of the member . 4.3. VOTE TO BE CAST: The owner or his/her proxy holder shall cast the votes using electronic/non-electronic plaƞorm as decided by the General Body

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 8 4.4. METHOD OF VOTING: a) ElecƟons VoƟng shall be strictly by secret ballot. Electronic/Non-Electronic plaƞorm shall be used b) Minor Decisions during Annual General Body MeeƟngs Raising hands as a method of voƟng shall be resorted as decided by General Body c) Major Decisions during Annual General Body MeeƟngs VoƟng shall be strictly by secret ballot. Electronic/Non-Electronic plaƞorm shall be used 4.5. QUORUM: The quorum for every General Body MeeƟng, whether extra ordinary or annual shall be one third of the total number of members of the AssociaƟon aƩending through electronic and non-electronic plaƞorms. If there is no quorum as speciﬁed above, within 30 minutes from the appointed Ɵme for meeƟng as given in the noƟce, the members present, may adjourn the meeƟng to another date and Ɵme which shall not be earlier than 48 hours (2days). NoƟce for this adjourned meeƟng shall be put up on all electronic/non-electronic plaƞorms within 12 hours of adjourning the meeƟng. If in such an adjourned meeƟng also, no quorum is present, the members present in electronic and non-electronic plaƞorms consƟtuƟng not less than 30% members, shall form a quorum, and the meeƟng shall consider the business given in the noƟce. 4.6. DISQUALIFICATION: Member or proxy with payment arrears shall not be allowed a) to vote on the elecƟon to select the Managing CommiƩee b) to contest elecƟon c) to be co-opted 4.7. MEETINGS AND PROCEDURE: a) PLACE OF MEETING: The General Body MeeƟng of the AssociaƟon shall be preferably held at locaƟons close to UTOPIA layout. Electronic and non-electronic parƟcipaƟon facility shall be provided. b) ANNUAL GENERAL BODY MEETING: a) The ﬁrst Annual General Body MeeƟng shall be held within a period of six months from the date of registraƟon of the Society, on the date, Ɵme and place to be decided by the interim Managing CommiƩee. b) ThereaŌer Annual General Body meeƟng of the AssociaƟon shall be held not later than three months from the close of each ﬁnancial year or such extended date as may be decided by the Managing CommiƩee in oﬃce. c) The agenda of the meeƟng (Annual General Body) shall be circulated to all the site/plot/house owners at least one month in advance. The site/plot/house

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 9 owners may also transact such other business of the AssociaƟon as may be placed before them. d) Probable ElecƟon date(s) shall be proposed in Annual General Body MeeƟng. ElecƟon date shall be ﬁnalized through electronic/non-electronic voƟng. It is recommended that elecƟon shall be conducted outside of Annual General Body MeeƟng. e) Minimum 7 members in number shall be elected for Managing CommiƩee. f) Elected Managing CommiƩee shall be in oﬃce for a period of 2 years. c) EXTRA ORDINARY GENERAL BODY MEETING: a) It shall be the duty of the President to call for an Extra Ordinary General Body MeeƟng of the AssociaƟon as directed by a resoluƟon of the Managing commiƩee or upon a peƟƟon through electronic/non-electronic plaƞorm by one-third of the members and having been presented to the Secretary. b) The noƟce of any Extra Ordinary General Body MeeƟng shall state the Ɵme and place of such meeƟng and purpose thereof. c) No other business shall be transacted at the meeƟng except what is stated in the noƟce. d) Secretary shall propose the date, Ɵme and venue to the Managing CommiƩee withing 7 days of receipt of peƟƟon. e) Managing CommiƩee shall ﬁnalize the date of Extra Ordinary General Body MeeƟng which shall be within 14 days from the date of Secretary’s proposal. d) NOTICE OF MEETING: It shall be the duty of the Secretary to send a noƟce of the meeƟng through electronic /non-electronic plaƞorm staƟng the purpose of the meeƟng as well as the Ɵme and place where it is to be held, to each resident/site owner(s), at least 21 days in advance in case of Annual General Body MeeƟng and at least 7 days in advance in case of Extra Ordinary General Body MeeƟng. e) SPECIAL MEETING OF MANAGING COMMITTEE: a) Special MeeƟng of the Managing CommiƩee may be called by the President by giving three days’ noƟce to each Managing CommiƩee members, staƟng the Ɵme, place and purpose of the meeƟng. b) The President or Secretary shall call such special meeƟngs of the Managing CommiƩee on like manner and on like noƟce (electronic/non-electronic) of at least two Managing CommiƩee members. f) PRESIDENT OF MEETING: a) The President or in his/her absence the Secretary shall preside at the Annual General Body MeeƟng/Extra Ordinary General Body MeeƟng. b) In the absence of both (i.e. the President and Secretary), the members present may choose a chairman from among themselves to preside over the meeƟng and conduct the proceedings.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 10 g) ORDER OF BUSINESS: The General Body is supreme and its decision binds all the members and shall be implemented and is empowered with authority vested in it by the consƟtuƟon and Bye-Laws of the AssociaƟon. The order of business at all meeƟng of the owners/members of site/plot/house shall be as follows: (i) Proof of noƟce of meeƟng or waiver of noƟce (ii) Reading of meeƟng Agenda (iii) Reading of minutes of previous meeƟng (iv) Reports of Managing CommiƩee including accounts for approval (v) Reports of CommiƩees (if any) and their adopƟon (vi) Proposal of Date of ElecƟon to select Managing CommiƩee (once in two years) (vii) PresentaƟon and adopƟon of proposed budget for the following year (viii) Performance of employees /staﬀ of associaƟon (ix) Revisit Welfare Service Charges (x) Any Other Business / MaƩers All the resoluƟons that have been passed in Annual General Body MeeƟng will become the part of the rules & regulaƟons for implementaƟon & conduct. III. GOVERNANCE AND GENERAL BODY 5. MATTERS TO BE DEALT WITH BY GENERAL BODY: 5.1. PRINCIPLES OF DECISION-MAKING: a) Given the mixed composiƟon of the Layout, comprising both vacant plots and houses, and to ensure parƟcipaƟon of all members, all policy decisions shall be taken by secured electronic voƟng open to all members based on the eligibility list maintained under these Bye-Laws. b) ParƟcipaƟon metrics shall be maintained to show the composiƟon of parƟcipaƟon (Plot owners vis a vis House Owners) to ascertain the fairness of the decisions c) A resoluƟon shall pass only with the approval of majority of the total voƟng strength (Polled). 5.2. ULTIMATE AUTHORITY: The ulƟmate authority in all maƩers concerning the aﬀairs, governance, and implementaƟon of the AssociaƟon shall vest in the General Body. 5.3. MATTERS EXCLUSIVELY WITHIN THE GENERAL BODY’S JURISDICTION: The following maƩers shall be dealt with exclusively by the General Body: a) Annual Accounts – ConsideraƟon and approval. b) Appointments – Appointment or re-appointment of auditors and legal advisors. c) Annual Budget – Approval for the succeeding year as presented by the Managing CommiƩee.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 11 d) ElecƟons – ElecƟon of Managing CommiƩee Members through secret electronic/non- electronic ballot, under the supervision of a Returning Oﬃcer and neutral observers proposed by Advisory CommiƩee e) Accounts and Audit –  The AssociaƟon shall close its accounts on 31 March each year.  Audited accounts shall be completed and published within 3 months of year-end.  Statements shall include: o Income and expenditure with reference to lawful approvals. o Assets and liabiliƟes, with disclosure of valuaƟon methods for ﬁxed assets. o Complete list of site and house owners/members as at year-end. o Date up to which proﬁts and expenses o Budget vs Actual o Extra Ordinary Expenses o Expenses against Corpus  Audited ﬁnancial statements shall be open for inspecƟon for any member in the associaƟon oﬃce. Copies (electronically or non-electronic) shall be provided free or at actual reproducƟon cost within 7 working days of request.  All documents shall be published electronically and shared in a folder/ applicaƟon. Member shall be able to access easily. 5.4. COMMITTEES APPOINTED BY THE GENERAL BODY: All commiƩees described below shall consist of eligible members, none of whom are on the Management CommiƩee, and shall maintain proporƟonal representaƟon of plot and house owners or as decided by General Body. a) Appointments CommiƩee Recommends all staﬀ appointments (e.g., Manager/Supervisor) based on General Body approved criteria covering remuneraƟon, qualiﬁcaƟons, experience, age and gender. b) Ethics (Enquiry) CommiƩee (Internal or External) InvesƟgates and reports on speciﬁc maƩers, relying on documentary and record evidence, within a set Ɵme-frame. c) Grievance CommiƩee Receives, reviews, and resolves member grievances in a fair, Ɵmely, and transparent manner, making recommendaƟons to the Managing CommiƩee or General Body as required. Report with metrics shall be circulated to the members. d) Internal Audit CommiƩee Audits the compliance against Bye-Laws, Management and AdministraƟon Processes of the AssociaƟon. At least once in a year, internal audit shall be performed. Results shall be reported to General Body in Annual General Body MeeƟng.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 12 e) Welfare Service CommiƩee Studies, proposes and implements a standard model for associaƟon’s welfare services like repair, security, water, electricity and upkeep etc. f) Advisory CommiƩee (Internal/External) Advises and guides - on elecƟon process including appointment of neutral observers, on handover and takeover from/to Managing CommiƩees and on managing the associaƟon maƩers. Advisory CommiƩee members shall be from previous managing commiƩees and/or subject maƩer experts from amongst the members or external. 6. ELECTION OF MANAGING COMMITTEE: a. ElecƟon shall be conducted like Public Oﬃce elecƟons to ensure fair pracƟce and full transparency. Any deviaƟons due to exigent circumstances shall be approved by General Body before implementaƟon. b. Managing CommiƩee shall have proporƟonate representaƟon between plot owners & house owners Ɵll the plot owners and house owners are equal in number. Post which the representaƟon shall be 3 or 4 plot owners and house owners with the President term alternaƟng between plot owners and house owners. c. If proporƟonal representaƟon cannot be achieved due to lack of nominaƟons, the Annual General Body, in its meeƟng shall decide the commiƩee composiƟon based on the available nominaƟons and elecƟon outcomes d. ElecƟons shall be conducted by secret ballot through a secured electronic/non-electronic voƟng plaƞorm, with a polling period of seven (7) days, allowing parƟcipaƟon of all eligible members. e. Only primary (ﬁrst) owner/member of each residenƟal unit or plot, as recorded in the AssociaƟon’s Membership Register, shall be eligible to contest for the post of the oﬃce bearers and commiƩee members. 6.1. ELECTION AND TERMS OF OFFICE: a. The term of oﬃce of the Managing CommiƩee elected shall be for 2 years only and counted collecƟvely (both primary and joint owners). The Managing CommiƩee shall hold oﬃce unƟl their successors are elected. The ﬁrst Managing CommiƩee was appointed through online meeƟng on 05-Sep-2025 (Refer: Appendix I) b. The President/Secretary shall announce the date of elecƟon, Ɵme and venue & of the to the members with a minimum noƟce of 30 days along with the name of returning oﬃcer and neutral observers. c. The Secretary shall publish the eligible list to the members for nominaƟons with a copy to the returning oﬃcer . d. NominaƟons shall be sent to the returning oﬃcer within 7 days of the elecƟon announcement through electronic/non-electronic) mode. e. The nominaƟon shall be proposed and seconded by eligible members using electronic/non-electronic mode. f. Every eligible member shall be enƟtled to nominate only one person.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 13 g. Every nominaƟon must state clearly the name, phase, plot/house number reference, address & occupaƟon and shall be signed (physical or digital signature) by the member staƟng willingness to act on the Managing commiƩee using the NominaƟon Form. h. The nominaƟons so received from the members shall be kept in safe custody by the returning oﬃcer who shall then publish the list of contestants at least 14 days before the date of elecƟon to the members. i. All elecƟons of the Managing commiƩee shall be held by secret ballot using electronic/non electronic plaƞorm to elect minimum 7 commiƩee members. j. Returning Oﬃcer shall conduct the elecƟon as per the process sƟpulated in the Bye- Laws and announce the results to all the members through electronic mode on the last day of the elecƟon. All documentaƟon shall be handed over to the Managing CommiƩee for record keeping purpose on compleƟon of elecƟon process in a sealed envelope. 7. POWERS AND DUTIES OF THE MANAGING COMMITTEE: 7.1. GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES: a) The Managing CommiƩee shall ensure clean, transparent, and fair administraƟon of the AssociaƟon, in compliance with applicable laws and this Bye-Laws. It shall act in the best interest of members, ensuring cost-eﬀecƟve and eﬃcient operaƟons. b) The Managing CommiƩee shall communicate all occurrences of any kind to the members as soon as it occurs along with corresponding acƟons. 7.2. SPECIFIC POWERS AND DUTIES: The Managing CommiƩee shall be responsible for: a) PromoƟng goodwill, fair pracƟces, and transparency in line with the AssociaƟon’s objecƟves. b) Supervising associaƟon staﬀ to ensure integrity, eﬃciency, and accountability. c) Admiƫng new members whose properƟes fall within the land survey numbers deﬁned in these Bye-Laws. d) Ensuring that Welfare Service Charges are levied, collected and revised proporƟonately & periodically between plot owners and house owners, based on consumpƟon of services and as agreed in General Body. e) Convening Annual and Extra Ordinary General Body MeeƟngs. f) Ensuring the ﬁnancial audit compleƟon on Ɵme g) Ensuring that the Audited Financial Statement in Annual General Body MeeƟng along with Auditor Report is presented by the Auditor h) PresenƟng the annual budget to the General Body. i) Recommending staﬀ appointments, terminaƟon and remuneraƟon (via the Appointments CommiƩee) subject to General Body approval. j) Removing any Managing CommiƩee member and ﬁlling the vacancy through co- opƟng via electronic voƟng.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 14 k) Framing internal regulaƟons consistent with these Bye-Laws, to be recorded in the minutes and put to General Body vote for approval. l) Opening and operaƟng the AssociaƟon’s bank account, jointly by the President and one of the Managing CommiƩee members not being the Secretary and the Treasurer. m) Deﬁning audit and accounƟng procedures and ensuring Ɵmely compliance. n) InspecƟng ﬁnancial records and iniƟaƟng recovery of dues. o) SancƟoning the expenses as shown below. Enhancement of these powers shall be with the General Body # Sanctioning Authority Recurring Non-Recurring 1 Managing Committee - Rs 1 Lakh 2 President - Rs 50000 3 Secretary FULL Rs 10000 4 Treasurer - Rs 5000 p) Following the process of proposal and approval for all the expenses q) Ensuring the cash book is updated monthly and signed by an authorized Managing CommiƩee member/s. A scanned copy, along with bank details, shall be published to all members by the 5th of the following quarter. r) ArƟculaƟng all Corpus transacƟons clearly in a separate secƟon with reference to relevant approvals/authorizaƟons. 8. OTHER DUTIES: In addiƟon to the duƟes outlined in SecƟon 7 and those imposed by resoluƟons of the General Body, the Managing CommiƩee shall: a) Oversee the care, upkeep, and surveillance of all Welfare & common services and faciliƟes. b) Appoint the ﬁrst auditor of the AssociaƟon. c) Ensure that audit and accounƟng procedures are implemented as approved. d) Receive and resolve member complaints fairly, transparently, and in a Ɵmely manner. IV. OFFICE BEARERS AND ROLES 9. OFFICE BEARERS: The Managing CommiƩee of the AssociaƟon shall comprise of minimum 7 members that includes, the President, Secretary, Treasurer and commiƩee members all of whom shall be elected by the members. 10. PRESIDENT: The President shall be the Chief ExecuƟve of the AssociaƟon. He/she shall preside over all meeƟngs of the AssociaƟon and of the Managing CommiƩee. He/she shall have all the general powers and duƟes which are usually vested in the oﬃce of President of an AssociaƟon.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 15 10.1. POWER AND FUNCTION: a) The President shall preside over the enƟre meeƟng. b) In case of a Ɵe of the votes in any of the meeƟngs menƟoned above, the President shall have an addiƟonal vote for casƟng. c) The President together with the Secretary shall be responsible for the overall implementaƟon of the policies laid down by the Managing CommiƩee and General Body. d) The President shall ensure that the monthly earnings and monthly expenditure of the AssociaƟon are maintained at the sƟpulated level indicated earlier and take suitable acƟon forthwith, as speciﬁed therein. 11. SECRETARY: 11.1. POWER AND FUNCTION: a) The Secretary shall be primarily responsible for all relevant communicaƟon to the members b) The Secretary together with the President shall be responsible for the overall implementaƟon of the policies laid down by the Managing CommiƩee and General Body. c) The Secretary shall call for all meeƟngs of the Managing CommiƩee and General Body MeeƟng/Extra Ordinary General Body MeeƟng in consultaƟon with the President. d) The Secretary shall be the custodian of arƟcles, items, both movable and immovable belonging to the AssociaƟon. e) The Secretary shall, in consultaƟon with the President and with the Managing CommiƩee as and when required shall act as a pivot for all acƟviƟes of the AssociaƟon, including reporƟng, budgeƟng, purchasing, hiring expenditures of all sorts, all correspondence between the associaƟons and also with outsiders to comply with his/her various duƟes and responsibiliƟes enumerated above. f) The Secretary has to be vigilant on income and expenditures of the AssociaƟon and strictly ensure the sƟpulaƟon regarding economy with suitable acƟons as speciﬁed therein. g) The Secretary shall be the point of correspondence of the AssociaƟon and shall maintain and preserve all records of the AssociaƟon. h) The Secretary shall have the responsibility to prepare, publish and keep the record of the minutes of the proceedings of the Managing CommiƩee and General Body MeeƟngs. Such minutes shall be authenƟcated by the President/Chairman of the meeƟng. i) The Secretary shall have the responsibility to keep the documents, registers and records of the AssociaƟon in safe custody.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 16 j) The Secretary shall have the power to appoint or remove various staﬀ or employees of the associaƟon subjected to the approval of the same by the General body/Managing CommiƩee/Appointments CommiƩee. k) The Secretary & Treasurer and shall be responsible to check all the transacƟons which has ﬁnancial impacts before ﬁnal payments 12. TREASURER: a) The Treasurer shall be responsible for maintaining and audiƟng the funds of the AssociaƟon. He/she shall also be responsible for maintaining proper and accurate accounts of all receipts and disbursements belonging to the AssociaƟon. b) The Treasurer shall be responsible for the deposit of all monies and other valuables designated by the Managing CommiƩee to the credit of the AssociaƟon. c) The Treasurer and Secretary shall be responsible to check all the transacƟons which has ﬁnancial impacts before ﬁnal payments 13. MANAGER/SUPERVISOR: a) The AssociaƟon may employ a paid Manager/Supervisor at a salary or compensaƟon determined by a resoluƟon of the General Body and as governed by the budget of the same. b) The Manager’s/Supervisor’s employment/ recruitment shall be based on the documented Job descripƟon. c) The Manager’s/Supervisor’s duƟes shall include: supervision of Welfare service acƟviƟes, coordinaƟon with vendors and service providers, management of records, physical custodian of assets/stores/equipment’s and any other responsibiliƟes authorized by the Managing CommiƩee. d) The Manager/Supervisor shall report to the Secretary, and in his/her absence, reporƟng shall be delegated to one of the Managing CommiƩee members by the President. e) The Manager/Supervisor shall not incur any ﬁnancial liability or commit AssociaƟon funds without prior wriƩen/email approval from the Treasurer (or their authorized delegates). f) The Manager’s/Supervisor’s performance shall be reviewed at least once annually by the Managing CommiƩee. The performance results shall be documented. Performance results shall be reviewed by members in Annual General Body MeeƟng. V. MANAGING COMMITTEE COMPOSITION AND TRANSITIONS 14. VACANCIES IN THE MANAGING COMMITTEE: Vacancies caused by any reason shall be ﬁlled in by co-opƟon through nominaƟon and voƟng process. Members shall vote using electronic plaƞorm so that all the members across the globe can parƟcipate. Whoever gets maximum votes shall be co-opted. Managing CommiƩee shall facilitate the process.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 17 15. RESIGNATION: An elected Managing CommiƩee member may resign at any Ɵme by sending a leƩer of resignaƟon to the President or in his/her absence to the Secretary of the AssociaƟon, assigning reason for his/her inability to funcƟon to which he/she volunteered and accepted. The resignaƟon shall take eﬀect from the date of acceptance by the Managing CommiƩee or one month from tendering resignaƟon whichever is earlier. The resignaƟon informaƟon shall be published to the members as soon as the resignaƟon is accepted 16. REMOVAL OF MANAGING COMMITTEE MEMBER: a) Any Managing CommiƩee member who is a defaulter in paying Welfare Service Charges and any other dues, for a period of 3 consecuƟve months shall be removed automaƟcally. b) Any Managing CommiƩee member who  Has failed to aƩend 3 consecuƟve meeƟngs of the Managing CommiƩee without leave of absence  Has failed to funcƟon as per the approved decisions by Managing CommiƩee  Does not accept responsibility as approved and guided by Managing CommiƩee  Has been found of any misconduct shall be removed from the Managing CommiƩee subject to the approval of 2/3 of Managing CommiƩee members. c) The reasons shall be recorded with the evidences, signed by the Managing CommiƩee and communicated to the members. Removed member shall also have the right to communicate to the members about his views with evidences. 17. HANDING OVER CHARGES: a) Upon elecƟon of a new Managing CommiƩee, the outgoing Secretary and Treasurer shall, within 30 working days, hand over electronic/non-electronic custody and responsibility of all AssociaƟon records, documents, books of accounts, and cash/bank balances (as per the latest bank reconciliaƟon statement) to their respecƟve incoming counterparts. The President shall oversee for the smooth transiƟon. b) Any electronic arƟfacts like login id, passwords shall be handover to the successive/incoming respecƟve Managing CommiƩee member. c) The handover shall be conducted through a joint veriﬁcaƟon process using Handover and Takeover Process and Checklist, by both outgoing and incoming oﬃcers, in the presence of Advisory CommiƩee. 18. REGULAR MANAGING COMMITTEE MEETING: a) Regular meeƟngs of the Managing CommiƩee shall be held at such Ɵme and place as shall be determined, from Ɵme to Ɵme. Such meeƟngs shall be held at least once in a month. The date shall be communicated to the General Body also. b) MeeƟng NoƟce shall be sent to all Managing CommiƩee members by Secretary at least 3 days before the meeƟng.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 18 c) The Secretary shall publish and maintain the minutes of the meeƟng of all such Managing CommiƩee meeƟngs in consultaƟon with the President within 7 days. d) MeeƟng shall be conducted through electronic/non-electronic mode. It is mandatory to share the minutes of meeƟng to all the members. e) MeeƟng By CirculaƟon - In case of urgency and circumstances so required, the commiƩee may decide on a maƩer by circulaƟng a note on the subject in respect of which each commiƩee member’s opinion may be sought which if implemented should be brought up for raƟﬁcaƟon of the commiƩee at its next meeƟng. f) Quorum - At all meeƟngs of the Managing CommiƩee, 5 members shall consƟtute a quorum for the transacƟon of business. If there be no quorum the meeƟng shall stand adjourned to another convenient Ɵme suitable to members without any further noƟce. g) Every subject/topic/issue not resolved shall be decided by a simple majority and in case of Ɵe, the President may exercise his/her vote. VI. FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT 19. CREATION OF FUNDS: 19.1. CORPUS FUND: a) The AssociaƟon shall maintain a Corpus Fund to meet the cost of: (i) ReconstrucƟon or major strengthening of buildings and common infrastructure, or (ii) Heavy repairs necessitated by events such as natural calamiƟes, riots, earthquakes, acts of terrorism, or other force majeure events. b) The Fund shall be built up through annual allocaƟons approved by the General Body. c) UƟlisaƟon of the Corpus Fund shall require approval by a two-thirds (Polled) majority of General Body through electronic voƟng with a window of 3 days. d) Welfare Service Charges shall be allocated as 15% for Corpus and remaining 85% towards the Welfare Services. e) UƟlizaƟon of Corpus Fund and Welfare Services Fund shall be reported separately in the Annual Finance statements. f) End of the ﬁnancial year, surplus shall be transferred to the Corpus Fund 19.2. WELFARE SERVICES FUND: a) Base Welfare Service Charge of Rs 1250/Month/Unit shall be contributed by all the members b) Any addiƟonal expenses shall be charged as AddiƟonal Welfare Service Charges -Split between Plots and Houses in a raƟo of 1:3 c) IllustraƟon of Base & AddiƟonal Welfare Service Charges shall be referred in Annexture II d) Following services shall be paid by the respecƟve owners menƟoned below: a. Personal Water and Electricity consumpƟon – Residents b. House hold Garbage collecƟon – Residents

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 19 c. Diesel Generator AMC/Repair and Fuel – Residents d. Repair & Maintenance cost of Pumps (treated water) & STP - Residents e) Hiring criteria for SecuriƟes shall be as below e. Up to 75 Units including 4 residents – 2 f. From 75 – 100 Units including 20 residents – 4 g. From 100 – 150 Units including 30 residents – 6 h. From 150 – 250 Units including 50 residents – 9 i. Above 100 residents – 15 (includes Security in charge) j. Maximum SecuriƟes allowed – 15 f) Hiring criteria for Employees shall be as below k. Up to 75 Units including 4 residents – 3 l. From 75 – 100 Units including 20 residents – 5 m. From 100 – 150 Units including 30 residents – 8 n. From 150 – 250 Units including 50 residents – 10 o. Above 100 residents – 11 (includes Manager/Supervisor) p. Maximum Employees allowed – 11 g) All funcƟons and celebraƟons cost shall be borne by those who opt to parƟcipate h) Welfare Service CommiƩee shall study, propose & execute services required for the associaƟon using the standard methods. i) For services with cost more than INR 1 Lakh, standard method of Request of QuotaƟon, Purchase Order, ExecuƟon and Acceptance shall be followed. j) The General Body shall ﬁx the rates for Welfare Services from Ɵme to Ɵme. Any change in rate must be discussed in General Body and approved by 75% of the members. Electronic voƟng shall be exercised for members approval. k) Welfare Service charges shall be payable in advance, annually and as determined by the General Body. l) Payments shall be made by cash, cheque, or approved digital transfer modes. m) A grace period of 30 days from the due date shall be allowed for members to pay, aŌer which a late fee at 12%/Annum simple interest rate or as determined by the General Body shall be levied. n) Members whose dues are in arrears can’t parƟcipate in any voƟng unƟl arrears are cleared. o) If arrears remain unpaid for a conƟnuous period of two years, the AssociaƟon may iniƟate legal proceedings for recovery. 19.3. Developer-Linked Club Membership The conƟnuaƟon of any membership beneﬁts provided by the developer shall be subject to the member’s Ɵmely payment of Welfare Service Charges, in line with any developer agreements in force. These provisions are administraƟve only and do not bind the AssociaƟon to obligaƟons beyond its control.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 20 20. ACCOUNT AND AUDIT: 20.1. BANK ACCOUNTS: a) The AssociaƟon shall maintain one or more accounts (savings and/or current) in a scheduled bank. b) Accounts shall be operated under joint signatures of any two of the following i.e the President & any Managing CommiƩee member not being the Treasurer and the Secretary c) All funds received shall be deposited promptly. d) PeƩy cash not exceeding ₹10,000 may be retained by the Secretary or Treasurer for immediate expenses, supported by receipts. e) Any Oﬃce-Bearer, Manager/Supervisor handling AssociaƟon funds shall maintain proper accounts and submit them monthly to the Treasurer. f) Excess funds may be placed in ﬁxed deposits or other safe instruments with a scheduled bank, as approved by the Managing CommiƩee. 20.2. PUBLICATION OF ACCOUNTS: A copy of the annual ﬁnancial statement and auditor’s report shall be displayed prominently at the AssociaƟon oﬃce and/or on its oﬃcial noƟce board (electronic/non- electronic) / website/ email. 20.3. APPOINTMENT OF AUDITOR: a) The AssociaƟon shall, at its Annual General Body MeeƟng, appoint an independent, qualiﬁed auditor for a term of one year. b) The auditor shall audit the AssociaƟon’s accounts, verify the annual return, and report to the Managing CommiƩee whether the accounts are correct, duly vouched, and in accordance with law. c) Any deﬁciencies shall be reported in wriƟng to the Managing CommiƩee. The same shall be shared to Members in Annual General Body MeeƟng. 20.4. POWER OF AUDITOR: a) The auditor may call for and examine any books, records, or documents relaƟng to the AssociaƟon’s ﬁnances and common faciliƟes. b) The auditor may make special reports to the Managing CommiƩee on any maƩer requiring aƩenƟon. c) The auditor shall be enƟtled to aƩend the Annual General Body MeeƟng to present audit observaƟons and answer members queries.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 21 VII. LEGAL, OPERATIONAL OVERSIGHT , AMENDMENTS & DISSOLUTION 21. APPOINTMENT OF LEGAL ADVIOSR: The AssociaƟon shall when the circumstances so demand appoint a qualiﬁed legal advisor to deal with issues requiring such services in the interest of the associaƟon and its members from Ɵme to Ɵme as decided by General Body. Such appointments will be made on terms and condiƟons and on such remuneraƟon and for such speciﬁc period as may be decided by the Managing CommiƩee. 22. THE WORKING HOURS OF THE ASSOCIATION: Morning 09:00 AM to Evening 06:00 PM 23. MISCELLENEOUS MATTERS: 23.1. SEAL OF THE ASSOCIATION: The AssociaƟon shall have a common seal which shall be in the custody of the President/Secretary and shall be used only under the authority of a resoluƟon of the AssociaƟon and every deed of instrument, to which the seal is aﬃxed, shall be aƩested for as on behalf of the AssociaƟon by two members of the Managing CommiƩee, one of them being the President/Secretary or any other person authorized by the AssociaƟon on that behalf. 23.2. PENALTY FOR BREACH OF RULES: The General Body of the AssociaƟon may prescribe for diﬀerent breaches of the rules and regulaƟons of the AssociaƟon, in respect of which the Managing CommiƩee shall be responsible to ensure compliance. Electronic/non-electronic plaƞorm shall be used for voƟng so that all the owners/members across the globe can parƟcipate. 23.3. INSPECTION OF BOOKS AND RECORDS: Any member shall have the right to inspect free of cost books, registers and documents, minutes, etc related to the aﬀairs of the AssociaƟon and get copies of documents of payment of fees as may be prescribed by the Managing CommiƩee. 23.4. BOOKS OF GUIDELINES: All members shall follow the do’s and don’ts detailed in the book of guidelines supplied by the AssociaƟon, in so far it is consistent with the normal code of discipline and do not encroach upon the legal rights of the owners. 23.5. SUE AND BE SUED: The AssociaƟon being jurisdicƟonal person can sue in its own name represented by the President or Secretary in all common causes whenever a legal acƟon is found necessary, the members of the AssociaƟon jointly act and insƟtute through the AssociaƟon represenƟng them and not individually or separately. This however is not applicable for the dispute between the members or of personal nature where they go for arbitraƟon or other lawful remedies.

New Utopia AssociaƟon Bye Laws - Version 1 Page No. 22 24. AMENDMENTS OF BYELAWS: a) AlteraƟon or amendment of the Memorandum of AssociaƟon, Bye Laws (Rules and RegulaƟons) shall be made as per Sec 9 and 10 of the KSR Act 1960. Bye Laws may be amended by the associaƟon in a duly consƟtuted meeƟng for the purposes. No amendment shall take place unless approved by members represenƟng at least 75% of the total as shown in the Memorandum of AssociaƟon. Electronic/non-electronic plaƞorm shall be used for approval so that all the members across the globe can parƟcipate. b) Every change in the Memorandum of AssociaƟon, Bye Laws (Rules and RegulaƟons) shall be ﬁled with the Registrar of SocieƟes within ﬁŌeen days from the date of making thereof. 25. DISSOLUTION: If the dissoluƟon of the AssociaƟon arises Sec 22 and 23 of the KSR Act 1960 shall be followed. 26. AMALGAMATION: Regarding the AmalgamaƟon of the AssociaƟon arises Sec 21 of the KSR Act 1960 shall be followed. For the maƩer, which have not been speciﬁed, provided to therein above the provisions of the KSR Act 1960 and the rules made there under shall apply. 27. SPIRIT OF THE ASSOCIATION As New Utopia AssociaƟon, a registered and independent society for the purpose of administraƟon of its safety, security and welfare, the main resolve is to manage and operate in a transparent, fair and eﬃcient manner, where all owners feel duty bound to pay for services and live in peace, harmony, courtesy and joy. ______________________END OF THE DOCUMENT_________________________________
